message:
fix(resolv): inet_neta walks bytes high-to-low to match libresolv integer contract

The previous implementation in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs`
walked `src` from low byte to high byte (`src & 0xff`, `src >>= 8`).
That works if you assume `in_addr_t` is laid out so the first dotted-
quad octet is the low byte — which is the network-byte-order layout
on a little-endian host. But libresolv's actual `inet_neta` contract
treats `src` as a host-side integer where the first octet is the
*most-significant* non-zero byte: `0xc0a80100` formats as
`"192.168.1"`, not `"0.1.168.192"`. Calling code that already has a
host-order address (e.g. via `ntohl()`) would therefore see a
reversed result.

Switch to the high-to-low walk:

- Read `b = (src >> 24) as u8` and shift `src <<= 8` each iteration.
- Build the result into a 16-byte stack `tmp` buffer first, only
  emitting a `'.'` separator *between* consecutive emitted octets
  (cleanly handles the leading-zero-skip case where the first
  surviving octet must not be preceded by a dot).
- After the loop, write a single trailing `\0`, perform one
  `cursor + 1 > size` check, and `copy_nonoverlapping` the
  populated prefix into `dst`. This collapses the previous
  per-iteration `cursor + 5 > size` worst-case check into a single
  exact-size check at the end, so callers that pass a
  `"192.168.1.5\0"`-sized buffer (12 bytes) succeed instead of
  failing the loose worst-case bound.
- The `src == 0` short-circuit pre-populates `tmp[..7]` with
  `b"0.0.0.0"` and sets `cursor = 7` so the same trailing-NUL +
  size-check path produces `"0.0.0.0"` without a separate copy.

Doc comment and worked examples are rewritten to use hex literals
(`0x7f000000`, `0xc0a80100`, `0xc0a80105`) so the byte-order
contract is no longer implicit in the dotted-quad notation.

`crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/resolv_abi_test.rs`:

- Flip the `na(a, b, c, d)` helper from `u32::from_le_bytes` to
  `u32::from_be_bytes` so dotted-quad call sites still encode the
  most-significant byte as the first dotted octet under the new
  contract.
- Add `inet_neta_formats_high_order_bytes_first` proving the raw
  hex inputs `0x0001_a8c0` and `0x0000_007f` format as
  `"1.168.192"` and `"127"` respectively.
- Add `inet_neta_accepts_exact_size_buffer` proving a 4-byte
  buffer fits `"127\0"` and a 12-byte buffer fits
  `"192.168.1.5\0"` — exact-fit cases that the old loose +5 bound
  rejected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
